creator: jt@obs-sec.com
text:
(In reply to Tobias C. Berner from comment #1)
(In reply to Tobias C. Berner from comment #2)
(In reply to Tobias C. Berner from comment #5)


When I compile locally:

[q5sys@skynet] /tmp/qt5ct-0.33% qmake-qt5 > qmake_output.txt
Project MESSAGE: Found lrelease executable: /usr/local/lib/qt5/bin/lrelease
Project MESSAGE: generating translations
Project MESSAGE: PREFIX=/usr
Project MESSAGE: BINDIR=/usr/bin
Project MESSAGE: DATADIR=/usr/share
Project MESSAGE: PLUGINDIR=/usr/local/lib/qt5/plugins
[q5sys@skynet] /tmp/qt5ct-0.33% make 
cd src/qt5ct-qtplugin/ && ( test -e Makefile || /usr/local/bin/qmake-qt5 /tmp/qt5ct-0.33/src/qt5ct-qtplugin/qt5ct-qtplugin.pro -o Makefile ) && make -f Makefile
Project MESSAGE: This project is using private headers and will therefore be tied to this specific Qt module build version.
Project MESSAGE: Running this project against other versions of the Qt modules may crash at any arbitrary point.
Project MESSAGE: This is not a bug, but a result of using Qt internals. You have been warned!
/usr/local/lib/qt5/bin/moc -DQT5CT_DATADIR=\"/usr/share\" -DQT_NO_DEBUG -DQT_PLUGIN -DQT_PLATFORMSUPPORT_LIB -DQT_WIDGETS_LIB -DQT_GUI_LIB -DQT_DBUS_LIB -DQT_CORE_LIB -I/usr/local/lib/qt5/mkspecs/freebsd-clang -I/tmp/qt5ct-0.33/src/qt5ct-qtplugin -I/tmp/qt5ct-0.33/src -I/usr/local/include/qt5 -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtPlatformSupport -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtPlatformSupport/5.7.1 -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtPlatformSupport/5.7.1/QtPlatformSupport -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtGui/5.7.1 -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtGui/5.7.1/QtGui -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtWidgets -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtGui -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtCore/5.7.1 -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtCore/5.7.1/QtCore -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtDBus -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtCore -I/usr/include/c++/v1 -I/usr/lib/clang/4.0.0/include -I/usr/include main.cpp -o .build/moc/main.moc
clang++ -c -pipe -O2 -std=gnu++11 -Wall -W -pthread -fPIC -DQT5CT_DATADIR=\"/usr/share\" -DQT_NO_DEBUG -DQT_PLUGIN -DQT_PLATFORMSUPPORT_LIB -DQT_WIDGETS_LIB -DQT_GUI_LIB -DQT_DBUS_LIB -DQT_CORE_LIB -I. -I.. -I/usr/local/include/qt5 -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtPlatformSupport -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtPlatformSupport/5.7.1 -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtPlatformSupport/5.7.1/QtPlatformSupport -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtGui/5.7.1 -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtGui/5.7.1/QtGui -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtWidgets -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtGui -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtCore/5.7.1 -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtCore/5.7.1/QtCore -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtDBus -I/usr/local/include/qt5/QtCore -I.build/moc -I/usr/local/include -I/usr/local/lib/qt5/mkspecs/freebsd-clang -o .build/obj/main.o main.cpp
[q5sys@skynet] /tmp/qt5ct-0.33%

At least on my machine that variable is not being passed to the compiler, I'll check to see why that is.  Also my patch should be using XDG_DATA_[HOME/DIRS] instead of CONFIG... I can update my patch accordingly.

As for if this is needed if the .pri is passing the right arguments to the compiler... 

imho, the patch is still very useful/needed for standards support since the current functionality *works* but hard-coded paths are extremely bad and do not work for people that have different XDG dirs they switch between... hence the specifications.  

However, I do acknowledge that this 'should' be done upstream.  So if you'd rather not accept this and have me continue to push upstream that has currently ignored it, that's fine with me.  Ken Moore and I have decided to fork entirely for the Lumina-Desktop, so any changes we need... we can implement.
